reasoning: The baseline price for April 13, 2025 (a Sunday) is the close on Friday, April 11, which was 1888.45. FICO experienced significant volatility recently, dropping into the 1600s in early April amidst a call for a DOJ antitrust investigation by Senator Josh Hawley concerning its pricing practices. Although the stock rebounded sharply back to 1888.45, the recent 60-day linear trend remains negatively sloped, and the ongoing regulatory risk presents a real overhang—especially since FICO trades at a premium valuation (P/E ~75). Given the recent bounce has just reclaimed the drop level, resistance around 1889 makes a short-term fallback slightly more likely than an immediate breakout. Thus, I assign a sub-50% probability (0.46) for the near-term (April 20). Expectedly, earnings in late April may clarify the regulatory impact, but downside macro/regulatory risks slightly outweigh upside for now. Per instructions, uncertainty increases over time, so probabilities for longer-term horizons gradually revert to 0.50, reflecting the increasing randomness of long-horizon stock price movements compared to a fixed today-price threshold.
